title: christella glanduligera
intro: Christella glanduligera, also known as the "glandular thyme fern," is a type of fern native to tropical Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Christella glanduligera include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Christella glanduligera can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions.
